steps.conclusion: The value proposition could be slightly more learner-facing and idea-driven, closer to the expected tone of turning ideas into websites or apps people enjoy using, while keeping the current concrete focus on responsive, accessible interfaces.

testCase.expectations:
- Should position frontend development as practical interface and web-product work
      - valueProposition should feel concrete and learner-facing, similar to "Turn your ideas into great websites and apps people will love to use"
      - valueProposition should not use abstract phrasing like "turns ideas into usable web experiences people can interact with"
      - audience should include beginners and people collaborating with product/design/engineering teams
      - outcomes should focus on usable interfaces, responsive pages, accessibility, and improving real web experiences
      - opportunities should mention realistic work environments without salary or job guarantees
      - Should be in US English

      
  - Must be written in the specified language
  - Must include exactly these fields: valueProposition, audience, outcomes, opportunities
  - valueProposition must be one concise sentence
  - audience must contain 3-5 short, specific learner profiles or fit statements
  - audience entries may be concise labels such as "People preparing for medical school" when they clearly answer who the course is for
  - outcomes must contain 4-6 concrete abilities learners can build
  - opportunities must contain 3-5 realistic career, academic, life, or cultural opportunities
  - Must be specific to the course topic, not generic education copy
  - valueProposition must sound natural and concrete, not like an abstract definition of the field
  - For non-English cases, must not mix in English audience or framing words such as "learners", "students", "beginners", or "professionals"; translate them naturally
  - Must not mention course duration, completion time, salaries, certificates, job guarantees, guaranteed fluency, pricing, subscriptions, or free chapters
  - Must avoid inflated claims such as "master", "become an expert", and "unlock your potential"

chapters.description: Set up a browser, editor, local files, and developer tools so you can inspect pages, run small changes, and read errors without guessing.
chapters.title: Frontend development setup

chapters.description: Use semantic HTML, document structure, links, forms, media, and landmarks to create pages that browsers and assistive technologies can understand.
chapters.title: HTML structure and semantic markup

chapters.description: Control typography, spacing, color, the cascade, selectors, custom properties, and responsive sizing without fighting the browser.
chapters.title: CSS foundations

chapters.description: Build layouts with normal flow, flexbox, grid, positioning, and container-aware decisions that work across phones, tablets, and desktops.
chapters.title: Responsive layout systems

chapters.description: Run JavaScript in the browser, read and change DOM nodes, handle events, and update the interface from user actions.
chapters.title: JavaScript for interactive pages

chapters.description: Collect user input with accessible labels, validation, disabled states, error messages, and feedback that helps people recover.
chapters.title: Forms, validation, and feedback

chapters.description: Manage focus, keyboard paths, ARIA used only where needed, reduced motion, contrast, and screen reader-friendly updates.
chapters.title: Accessibility and inclusive interaction

chapters.description: Organize interfaces with reusable components, props, state, rendering cycles, and composition patterns used by modern frontend frameworks.
chapters.title: Component-based UI architecture

chapters.description: Load data with fetch, handle loading and error states, parse JSON, update UI from APIs, and protect users from stale or missing data.
chapters.title: API data and async UI states

chapters.description: Use package management, bundlers, dev servers, TypeScript, linting, formatting, and build outputs with a clear mental model.
chapters.title: Frontend tooling and TypeScript

chapters.description: Profile rendering, layout shifts, bundle size, network waterfalls, image loading, and Core Web Vitals before shipping changes.
chapters.title: Frontend performance

chapters.description: Take a feature from user need through layout, component design, states, accessibility checks, tests, build, and deployment handoff.
chapters.title: End-to-end frontend feature workflow

userInput.description: Frontend development powers the visual and interactive layer of modern web products.
userInput.title: Frontend Development

chapters.description: Polite greetings, farewells, introductions, and basic courtesy formulas with formal and informal address.
chapters.title: Greetings and introductions

chapters.description: Subject pronouns, gender, articles, and simple affirmative or negative sentences in everyday contexts.
chapters.title: Nouns, gender, and basic sentences

chapters.description: Numbers, prices, dates, time expressions, and talking about schedules and frequency.
chapters.title: Numbers, time, and dates

chapters.description: Regular and high-frequency irregular verbs in the present tense, with common verb patterns and spelling changes.
chapters.title: Present tense

chapters.description: Family members, relationships, basic personal descriptions, and possessive determiners.
chapters.title: Family and possession

chapters.description: Adjective agreement and position, common descriptive vocabulary, and comparative forms.
chapters.title: Descriptions and adjectives

chapters.description: Common questions with est-ce que, inversion, intonation, question words, and short answers.
chapters.title: Asking questions

chapters.description: Negation patterns such as ne pas, ne plus, and ne jamais, plus essential sentence-building connectors.
chapters.title: Negation and simple connectors

chapters.description: Food items, ordering and paying, quantities, partitive articles, and expressing preferences.
chapters.title: Food and dining

chapters.description: Daily routines, telling time in context, reflexive verbs, and frequency adverbs.
chapters.title: Daily life and routines

chapters.description: Places in town, asking for and giving directions, and prepositions of place.
chapters.title: Around town and directions

chapters.description: Transportation modes, tickets, schedules, and expressing destinations and itineraries.
chapters.title: Transportation and getting around

chapters.description: Body parts, symptoms, pharmacies, appointments, and giving basic health information.
chapters.title: Health and the body

chapters.description: Past narration with passe compose and imparfait, including sequence, background, and common irregular participles.
chapters.title: Telling stories in the past

chapters.description: Subjunctive triggers, wishes, doubt, emotion, necessity, and dependent clauses used in natural conversation.
chapters.title: Present subjunctive

chapters.description: Constrói o primeiro modelo mental de IA: dados, regras, atributos, rótulos, previsões, erros e decisões em sistemas que pessoas realmente usam.
chapters.title: Dados, regras e previsões

chapters.description: Acompanha a IA desde sistemas simbólicos e regras de especialistas até aprendizado de máquina, deep learning e modelos de fundação.
chapters.title: Evolução da inteligência artificial

chapters.description: Transforma um pedido real em problema de previsão, classificação, ranqueamento, geração, planejamento ou automação com medidas claras de sucesso.
chapters.title: Formulação de problemas de IA

chapters.description: Cobre coleta, limpeza, rotulagem, documentação e divisão de bases de dados, evitando vazamento, vieses escondidos e métricas fora da tarefa real.
chapters.title: Coleta e preparação de dados

chapters.description: Treina e compara regressão, classificação, clusterização, árvores e modelos ensemble com métricas ligadas ao custo dos erros.
chapters.title: Modelos clássicos de aprendizado de máquina

chapters.description: Usa redes neurais, backpropagation, otimização, GPUs e frameworks de deep learning para modelar imagens, texto, áudio e dados estruturados.
chapters.title: Redes neurais e deep learning

chapters.description: Trabalha atenção, transformers, embeddings, pré-treinamento, ajuste por instruções e limites dos grandes modelos de linguagem.
chapters.title: Transformers e grandes modelos de linguagem

chapters.description: Desenha prompts, saídas estruturadas, chamadas de ferramentas, versionamento e testes para deixar o comportamento da IA mais fácil de manter.
chapters.title: Prompting e design de aplicações de IA

chapters.description: Constrói geração aumentada por recuperação com ingestão de documentos, chunking, embeddings, busca vetorial, reranking, montagem de contexto e citações.
chapters.title: Geração aumentada por recuperação

chapters.description: Avalia IA com baselines, dados separados, revisão humana, juízes automáticos, red teaming, análise de alucinações e testes de regressão.
chapters.title: Avaliação de IA e análise de alucinações

chapters.description: Verifica justiça, privacidade, consentimento, explicabilidade, model cards, riscos de uso indevido e governança antes que sistemas de IA afetem pessoas.
chapters.title: IA responsável e governança

chapters.description: Implanta recursos de IA com serving de modelos, monitoramento, fallbacks, orçamento de latência, controle de custo, resposta a incidentes e revisão humana.
chapters.title: Operação e implantação de IA

chapters.description: Acompanha um recurso de IA da formulação do problema até checagem de dados, desenho de modelo ou prompt, avaliação, lançamento, monitoramento e iteração.
chapters.title: Fluxo completo de produto com IA

userInput.description: A inteligência artificial transforma dados, regras e modelos em sistemas capazes de apoiar decisões.
userInput.title: Inteligência Artificial

chapters.description: Build the vocabulary needed to read statutes, contracts, cases, and legal news, including rights, duties, liability, jurisdiction, validity, and remedies.
chapters.title: Essential legal language

chapters.description: See how everyday events become legal facts, claims, defenses, obligations, and consequences across contracts, harm, property, work, and crime.
chapters.title: Legal rules, facts, and relationships

chapters.description: Compare constitutions, statutes, regulations, precedent, administrative rules, treaties, and legal customs so you can locate the governing authority.
chapters.title: Sources of law

chapters.description: Map courts, legislatures, agencies, prosecutors, defenders, private lawyers, clerks, and enforcement bodies in the path of a legal problem.
chapters.title: Legal systems and institutions

chapters.description: Trace how legal systems developed through common law, civil law, constitutionalism, codification, rights movements, regulation, and digital courts.
chapters.title: Legal history and institutional evolution

chapters.description: Read legal texts by language, context, purpose, precedent, principles, and practical consequences without treating interpretation as guesswork.
chapters.title: Legal interpretation

chapters.description: Identify issues, holdings, reasoning, dicta, precedent strength, factual distinctions, and how one case can support or weaken another argument.
chapters.title: Case reading and precedent

chapters.description: Build legal arguments from facts, rules, evidence, analogies, counterarguments, and conclusions while staying honest about uncertainty.
chapters.title: Legal reasoning and argumentation

chapters.description: Research statutes, cases, regulations, secondary sources, citators, and updates, then assemble a reliable answer with verifiable sources.
chapters.title: Legal research

chapters.description: Write clear memos, client letters, case briefs, contract notes, and procedural documents for readers who need to decide or act.
chapters.title: Legal writing

chapters.description: Follow a matter from intake through documents, deadlines, research, strategy, filing, negotiation, hearing, decision, enforcement, and follow-up.
chapters.title: End-to-end legal matter workflow

chapters.description: Cover confidentiality, conflicts of interest, unauthorized practice, supervision, legal advice boundaries, and professional responsibility in regulated settings.
chapters.title: Legal ethics and professional responsibility

userInput.description: A structured path through legal systems, case reading, legal reasoning, procedure, and professional responsibility.
userInput.title: Law

steps.conclusion: The outcome about applying pharmacology ideas to dosing could be read slightly too close to medication-management practice if taken out of context, though it does include safety and does not claim prescribing authority. The opportunity about understanding prescriptions and diagnoses is acceptable but could be phrased more clearly as understanding clinician-provided information.

Add a clearer supervised-study or non-licensure framing, such as preparing for medical or allied-health study and working better with qualified clinicians. Replace “health-care” with the more standard US spelling “healthcare” for consistency.

      - outcomes should focus on foundational medical knowledge, vocabulary, body systems, clinical reasoning context, and readiness for supervised study
      - Should be in US English

chapters.description: Build vocabulary for medical records, body regions, symptoms, pain, temperature, pulse, blood pressure, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and clinical measurement.
chapters.title: Medical terminology and vital signs

chapters.description: Connect cells, tissues, organs, vessels, nerves, and body regions to physical exams, imaging, procedures, and common clinical language.
chapters.title: Anatomy and histology

chapters.description: Study homeostasis, circulation, ventilation, kidney function, digestion, hormones, and neurologic control as the normal baseline for disease.
chapters.title: Physiology

chapters.description: Cover DNA, inheritance, mutations, genetic testing, enzymes, metabolism, acid-base balance, and the biochemical basis of disease and medication effects.
chapters.title: Genetics and medical biochemistry

chapters.description: Learn inflammation, immune responses, infection, microbiology, parasites, vaccines, allergies, autoimmunity, and immune deficiency.
chapters.title: Immunology and microbiology

chapters.description: Study cell injury, inflammation, thrombosis, necrosis, repair, neoplasia, and the mechanisms that turn normal function into disease.
chapters.title: Pathology

chapters.description: Connect pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, dosing, adverse effects, interactions, toxicity, and medication safety in supervised care contexts.
chapters.title: Pharmacology and safe medication use

chapters.description: Use incidence, prevalence, risk, bias, confounding, confidence intervals, diagnostic accuracy, and evidence appraisal in clinical decisions.
chapters.title: Epidemiology and evidence-based medicine

chapters.description: Practice communication, history taking, review of systems, physical examination, differential diagnosis, and clinical reasoning from symptoms and signs.
chapters.title: Anamnesis, physical exam, and clinical reasoning

chapters.description: Interpret common labs, pathology reports, radiography, ultrasound, CT, MRI, and bedside imaging with attention to indications and limits.
chapters.title: Laboratory and diagnostic imaging

chapters.description: Cover infection control, patient identification, medication reconciliation, informed consent, adverse events, documentation, privacy, and care handoffs.
chapters.title: Patient safety and clinical documentation

chapters.description: Follow a patient encounter from intake through history, exam, differential diagnosis, tests, treatment plan, counseling, documentation, and follow-up.
chapters.title: End-to-end clinical encounter workflow

chapters.description: Frame medical ethics, licensing rules, professional responsibility, prescribing limits, controlled substances, certificates, death documentation, and reporting duties.
chapters.title: Medical ethics, regulation, and professional duties

userInput.description: A beginner-to-advanced foundation in anatomy, physiology, pathology, pharmacology, clinical reasoning, and modern health care systems.

chapters.description: Place The Matrix in late-1990s cinema, cyberpunk fiction, hacker culture, anime influence, action filmmaking, and the rise of internet culture.
chapters.title: Cyberpunk and late-1990s cinema

chapters.description: Trace the Wachowskis' development process, casting, production design, training, choreography, visual effects planning, and release context.
chapters.title: Making The Matrix

chapters.description: Follow Neo, Trinity, Morpheus, Cypher, the Agents, and the film's core conflict from ordinary life to awakening and choice.
chapters.title: Story, characters, and the hero's path

chapters.description: Analyze simulation, reality, perception, free will, control, skepticism, and the philosophical questions the story makes visible.

chapters.description: Study bullet time, wire work, Hong Kong action influence, martial arts staging, editing rhythms, and how fights communicate character.
chapters.title: Action language and bullet time

chapters.description: Read costumes, color palettes, phones, code rain, sunglasses, architecture, and interfaces as part of the film's visual system.

chapters.description: Compare the sequels' expansion of Zion, machines, prophecy, choice, programs, Smith, and the trilogy's debated ending.
chapters.title: Reloaded, Revolutions, and trilogy structure

chapters.description: Connect The Animatrix, games, comics, and transmedia storytelling to canon, side stories, and how fans piece the world together.
chapters.title: Transmedia worldbuilding

chapters.description: Track influence on visual effects, games, fashion, music videos, internet aesthetics, political language, and later science fiction.
chapters.title: Cultural impact

chapters.description: Practice a complete media-analysis workflow: scene selection, close viewing, evidence notes, context research, argument drafting, and discussion.
chapters.title: End-to-end film analysis workflow
